name: Stewed Chicken (Pollo Estofado) 1911
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the chicken inside and out with salt and pepper.
2. Hold green bell peppers over a gas burner flame or broil them in the oven until the skins blister. Remove the skins and seeds, then chop the bell peppers, onion, and tomatoes.
3. Melt the lard in a 4-quart pot over medium-high heat. When hot, add the chicken to the pot and turn it to brown all sides for 5 to 6 minutes.
4. Add the chopped onions, tomatoes, and bell peppers to the pot and stir until everything is well-cooked, about 4 minutes.
5. Add red wine, sugar, raisins, olives, ground cloves, and salt and pepper to taste. Stir to mix well.
6. Pour in 2 cups of water and stir. Cover and cook over low heat until the juices run clear when the chicken is pierced with a fork (about 45 minutes). If needed, add more hot water during cooking.
7. Remove the chicken to a serving platter. Mix 2 tablespoons of flour with 3 tablespoons of water in a small bowl. Stir this mixture into the pan juices and bring it to a boil, stirring until thickened for 2 to 3 minutes.
8. Pour the sauce over the chicken and serve.
